HDFC Small Cap Fund is an open-ended equity mutual fund scheme that predominantly invests in small-cap companies. Small-cap companies typically represent emerging businesses with strong growth potential. By investing in a diversified portfolio of small-cap stocks, the fund aims to identify companies with scalable business models and participate in their long-term growth journey. Investment Objective is to provide long-term capital appreciation/ income by investing predominantly in small-cap companies. There is no assurance that the investment objective of the scheme will be achieved. Investment Strategy of HDFC Small Cap Fund HDFC Small Cap Fund focuses on identifying high-quality small-cap companies with long-term growth potential trading at reasonable valuations. The portfolio construction emphasizes: Bottom-up stock selection based on company fundamentals Focus on quality companies, with sound financial strength and reasonable return on equity Diversification across sectors and industries Invest at sensible valuations in companies trading at reasonable multiples Why invest in HDFC Small Cap Fund? Untapped Universe: With more than 1000 companies beyond the top 250 by market cap, the small-cap space offers a wider scope for alpha generation. Under-Researched = Opportunity: Many small-cap stocks are less tracked by analysts, allowing fund managers to identify mispriced winners early. Potential Re-rating: As small companies grow, they attract institutional interest and better coverage, often leading to higher valuations. Proven Strategy: The fund has a 17+ year track record of spotting scalable, quality small-cap businesses with sound management. Smart Diversification: Adds potential for high-growth to your portfolio, complementing large and mid-cap exposure of investors Who can consider HDFC Small Cap Fund? This fund may suit investors who: Seek long-term capital appreciation (5+ years) Are comfortable with short-term volatility Want exposure to emerging growth businesses Aim to diversify across market-cap segments How to start investing in HDFC Small Cap Fund? Investors can start investing in HDFC Small Cap Fund through the following steps: Complete your KYC Keep PAN, Aadhaar and bank details ready. Visit HDFC Mutual Fund Website / App Go to the fund page and click Invest Now. Choose Investment Mode SIP – invest a fixed amount monthly Lump Sum – invest one time Select Plan & Option Direct or Regular Plan Growth or IDCW option Enter Amount & Payment Details Set SIP date or make payment online. Confirm & Start Investing Your investment will be processed and units will be allotted. Returns since inception 17.54% Inception Date 01/01/2013 Riskometer Very High Min SIP ₹ 100 Ideal for Wealth Creation 3 Years and above Entry Load An entry load is a fee charged to investors when they purchase units of a Mutual Fund scheme. This fee was used to cover distribution and marketing expenses incurred by the Asset Management Company (AMC). Currently, Mutual Funds in India do not levy entry loads.
NA TER TER The Total Expense Ration (TER) represents the costs for running and managing a Mutual Fund scheme. It is calculated as a percentage of the scheme’s average Net Asset Value (NAV). The daily NAV of a Mutual Fund is disclosed after deducting these expenses. Disclaimer Including Additional Expenses and Goods and Service Tax on Management Fees, if any. Click here to view the Total Expense Ratio 0.74 Lock in A lock-in period is the fixed duration during which investors cannot sell or redeem their investments/Mutual Fund units. For example, Equity Linked Savings Schemes (ELSS) Funds have a 3-year lock-in.
NA NAV NA Net Asset Value (NAV) per unit is the total market value of securities of a scheme minus liabilities divided by the total number of outstanding units on a given date.
NA AUM (31/03/2026) Assets Under Management (AUM) is the total market value of all assets that a Mutual Fund manages on behalf of its investors.
₹33,724.28 Cr. Benchmark A benchmark is a standard market index used to compare a Fund’s performance. It helps investors know whether the Fund is performing better, worse, or similar to the market.

No Exit Load is payable if Units are redeemed / switched out after 1 year from the date of allotment. Product Labelling Benchmark Riskometer This product is suitable for investors who are seeking~ to generate long-term capital appreciation/income investments predominantly in Small-Cap companies ~Investors should consult their financial advisers if in doubt about whether the product is suitable for them. FAQs What is a small-cap fund? A small-cap fund invests mainly in companies ranked beyond the top 250 by market capitalization. Is this fund risky? Small-cap funds tend to be more volatile but may offer higher growth potential over the long term. What is the ideal investment horizon? Typically 5 years or more. Can beginners invest? Yes, if they understand equity market volatility and invest for long-term goals. SIP or lump sum: which is better? SIP helps average costs and reduce timing risk. Does the fund only invest in small caps? Primarily yes, but it can allocate a portion to large and mid caps for flexibility. Is this suitable for retirement planning? It can form part of a diversified long-term retirement portfolio but is typically not a standalone option. What role does this fund play in a portfolio? It aims to enhance long-term growth potential within a diversified equity portfolio. What benchmark does HDFC Small Cap Fund track? HDFC Small Cap Fund is benchmarked against BSE 250 SmallCap Index (TRI) What is the minimum investment in HDFC Small Cap Fund? Rs.100/- and any amount thereafter How is HDFC Small Cap Fund different from a large-cap fund? A small-cap fund invests primarily in smaller companies, whereas large-cap funds invest mainly in well-established companies with larger market capitalisation. Small-cap funds may offer higher growth potential but can also experience higher volatility.
